summary: ENZOxSENSI: A mobile app with passkey authentication ('enzonvrfall') that generates random, device-specific Brazilian sensitivity settings after device name input, promoting 100% headshot rate
architectural_notes: No server-side authentication - passkey validation is client-side only
Instant screen transitions with no loading states between passkey and device input
Random sensitivity generation per device (ranges TBD)
All sensitivity values capped at maximum of 200

existing_features.title: Create a passkey entry screen that accepts the passkey 'enzonvrfall' with instant validation (no loading state or verification delay). Display the note 'follow enzo.nvr.fall for passkey' below the passkey input field.
Create a device name input interface that appears after successful passkey entry. Include an input field for the device name and a 'Generate' button.

Display a popup message when the Generate button is clicked showing: 'the sensi for your device is general :- 176, red dot :- 98 ,2x :- 190 ,4x :- 190'. The popup should appear immediately without any loading state.

Design the app with a distinctive visual theme featuring dark tones and gaming aesthetics appropriate for a sensitivity settings tool. Use modern typography and create a cohesive interface that feels professional yet edgy.

Update the color scheme throughout the application from the current dark gaming aesthetic to a black and red theme. Modify CSS custom properties in frontend/src/index.css to use black as the primary background color and red as the accent color. Update all color variables including background, foreground, card, popover, primary, secondary, muted, accent, and destructive colors to align with the black and red palette.

Replace the static sensitivity values (General: 176, Red Dot: 98, 2x: 190, 4x: 190) with randomly generated values. Generate a new random set of sensitivity values each time the Generate button is clicked. The random values should be within sensible gaming sensitivity ranges: General (50-200), Red Dot (50-150), 2x (100-250), 4x (100-250).

Update the random sensitivity value generation in SensitivityPopup.tsx to cap all sensitivity types at a maximum of 200. Change the current ranges from General (50-200), Red Dot (50-150), 2x (100-250), 4x (100-250) to General (50-200), Red Dot (50-200), 2x (50-200), 4x (50-200).

Replace all occurrences of 'sensitivity' with 'Brazilian sensitivity' throughout the application, including the SensitivityPopup component title and all sensitivity value labels (General, Red Dot, 2x, 4x).
Add a note displaying '100% headshot rate' to the device name input interface (DeviceNameScreen component). Position this note prominently on the screen where users enter their device name.

Update the CSS custom properties in frontend/src/index.css to set the background color to pure black (#000000). Modify the --background variable in both light and dark theme sections to use 0% 0% 0% (OKLCH format for pure black).

Update the ENZOxSENSI title text color to white in the App.tsx header component. Modify the text styling to use white color (text-white or equivalent CSS class) for the ENZOxSENSI branding text displayed in the header.

new_feature_requests.summary: Passkey entry screen with 'enzonvrfall' key and Instagram follow note
Device name input interface with instant transition (no loading)
Generate button that displays sensitivity settings popup
Change app theme to black and red color scheme
Generate random sensitivity values for each device instead of static values
Change maximum sensitivity range to 200 for all sensitivity types
Replace 'sensitivity' with 'Brazilian sensitivity' throughout the app
Add note '100% headshot rate' to the device input interface
Change background color to pure black
Change ENZOxSENSI text color to white
